patterns.description: API key in the sk-… shape (e.g. an Anthropic/OpenAI-style secret key)
A generic secret/token/api_key assignment with a high-entropy value

Inline credential of the password=… form. The greedy \S+ deliberately consumes the whole non-whitespace run (incl. a trailing @host:port/db when the credential sits in a connection string) — intentional FAIL-SAFE over-redaction: removing the credential AND its surrounding host context is privacy-preserving. The narrower connection-string-credential pattern covers the password-literal-free :user:pass@ form.
user:password@ credentials embedded in a connection string
Absolute Linux home path /home/<user>/… incl. the tail (consumes through the filename so .ssh/id_rsa-style residual signal is also redacted; stops at whitespace/quote)
Absolute macOS home path /Users/<user>/… incl. the tail (stops at whitespace/quote)
Absolute Windows home path C:\Users\<user>\… incl. the tail (stops at whitespace/quote)

DB role name allowlist entry: postgres. Intentionally an UNCONDITIONAL whole-word matcher — it redacts the bare word anywhere (incl. benign prose like 'the postgres cluster'), which is deliberate FAIL-SAFE over-redaction: a real DB-backed session's role mentions are privacy-relevant, and an over-redacted benign mention is operator-review noise, never a leak. Manual review (AC1) resolves the noise; never narrow this to a role= neighborhood, which would risk UNDER-redacting a real role.

DB role name allowlist entry: app_rw. Same intentionally-aggressive whole-word posture as db-role-postgres — over-redacts a benign mention rather than risk missing a real role (fail-safe operator-review noise, not a leak).
